clusters.title: 6 killed after gunman opens fire in Kyiv, Ukrainian officials say
clusters.neutral_headline: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages
bullet_summary: The head of Ukraine's Interior Ministry said the gunman took hostages and spoke to negotiators for 40 minutes before he was shot dead
At least six people were killed in a shooting Saturday in a residential neighborhood of Kyiv, and 14 were hospitalized, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced
The suspected gunman was shot during an arrest attempt, officials said

items.title: 6 killed after gunman opens fire in Kyiv, Ukrainian officials say
items.standfirst: The head of Ukraine's Interior Ministry said the gunman took hostages and spoke to negotiators for 40 minutes before he was shot dead.
items.content: The head of Ukraine's Interior Ministry said the gunman took hostages and spoke to negotiators for 40 minutes before he was shot dead.

items.title: Ukrainian authorities investigating Kyiv mass shooting as a terror attack
items.standfirst: Ukrainian authorities are investigating a mass shooting as a terror attack in Kyiv that killed at least six people. The attacker, a local man, was killed in a shootout with police.
items.content: Ukrainian authorities are investigating a mass shooting as a terror attack in Kyiv that killed at least six people. The attacker, a local man, was killed in a shootout with police.

items.title: A mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves 6 dead before police shot and killed the gunman
items.standfirst: A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.
items.content: A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.(Image credit: Dan Bashakov)

items.title: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages

items.content: The attacker, who took hostages inside a supermarket, was killed after a shoot-out with police, officials say.

items.title: Ukraine police shoot dead gunman who killed six in Kyiv, took hostages
items.standfirst: The attacker has been identified as a "58-year-old Moscow man", but no motive has been established.
items.content: The attacker has been identified as a "58-year-old Moscow man", but no motive has been established.

items.title: At least six dead in Kyiv shooting, suspected gunman killed
items.standfirst: At least six people were killed in a shooting Saturday in a residential neighborhood of Kyiv, and 14 were hospitalized, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced. The suspected gunman was shot during an arrest attempt, officials said.
items.content: At least six people were killed in a shooting Saturday in a residential neighborhood of Kyiv, and 14 were hospitalized, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced. The suspected gunman was shot during an arrest attempt, officials said.

items.content: At least six people have been killed after a gunman opened fire in Kyiv.

clusters.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran says ‘fundamental’ issues’ still to be resolved with US amid strait of Hormuz impasse
clusters.neutral_headline: Iran closes strait of Hormuz again ‘until US lifts blockade’
bullet_summary: A UK maritime agency reported that Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) ships had fired at a tanker as it attempted to pass through the strait on Saturday
Reuters reported an Indian-flagged vessel carrying crude oil had also been attacked while in the waterway

items.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran says ‘fundamental’ issues’ still to be resolved with US amid strait of Hormuz impasse
items.standfirst: Iran’s top negotiator says both sides remain far apart after Tehran again closed strait of Hormuz after US said it would not end its blockadeFull report: Iran closes strait of Hormuz again ‘until US lifts blockade’Iranian president Masoud Pezeshkian was quoted in the state-affiliated Iranian Student News Agency on Sunday about Donald Trump and efforts to quash Iranian nuclear capabilities, Reuters reports.“Trump says Iran cannot make use of its nuclear rights but doesn’t say for what crime. Who is he to deprive a nation of its rights?” Pezeshkian was quoted as saying. Continue reading...
items.content: Iran’s top negotiator says both sides remain far apart after Tehran again closed strait of Hormuz after US said it would not end its blockadeFull report: Iran closes strait of Hormuz again ‘until US lifts blockade’Iranian president Masoud Pezeshkian was quoted in the state-affiliated Iranian Student News Agency on Sunday about Donald Trump and efforts to quash Iranian nuclear capabilities, Reuters reports.“Trump says Iran cannot make use of its nuclear rights but doesn’t say for what crime. Who is he to deprive a nation of its rights?” Pezeshkian was quoted as saying. Continue reading...

items.title: Middle East war live: Iran reports 'progress' in US talks but final deal still far off
items.standfirst: Iran’s parliamentary speaker said Saturday night that talks with the United States to end the war had made progress but remained far from an agreement, as Tehran said the crucial Strait of Hormuz would remain closed until Washington lifts its naval blockade on Iranian ports. Follow our liveblog to see how the events develop.
items.content: Iran’s parliamentary speaker said Saturday night that talks with the United States to end the war had made progress but remained far from an agreement, as Tehran said the crucial Strait of Hormuz would remain closed until Washington lifts its naval blockade on Iranian ports. Follow our liveblog to see how the events develop.

items.title: Iran closes strait of Hormuz again ‘until US lifts blockade’
items.standfirst: IRGC reportedly fires on tanker as it tries to pass through strait during brief window when shipping lane had reopenedAnalysis: Trump and Tehran’s series of mismanaged posts stall progress towards peace Iranian officials say they have reversed the reopening of the strait of Hormuz and reimposed restrictions on the vital shipping lane after the US said it would not end its blockade of Iranian ports.A UK maritime agency reported that Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) ships had fired at a tanker as it attempted to pass through the strait on Saturday. Reuters reported an Indian-flagged vessel carrying crude oil had also been attacked while in the waterway. Continue reading...
items.content: IRGC reportedly fires on tanker as it tries to pass through strait during brief window when shipping lane had reopenedAnalysis: Trump and Tehran’s series of mismanaged posts stall progress towards peace Iranian officials say they have reversed the reopening of the strait of Hormuz and reimposed restrictions on the vital shipping lane after the US said it would not end its blockade of Iranian ports.A UK maritime agency reported that Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) ships had fired at a tanker as it attempted to pass through the strait on Saturday. Reuters reported an Indian-flagged vessel carrying crude oil had also been attacked while in the waterway. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran says no date set for US talks, Hormuz Strait closed
items.standfirst: IRGC says the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed until the US stops blockading Iranian ports.
items.content: IRGC says the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed until the US stops blockading Iranian ports.

items.title: Trump and Tehran’s series of mismanaged posts stall progress towards peace
items.standfirst: US president’s desperation for war to end has seen him trying to speed through a process he does not fully controlA set of mismanaged and premature media announcements by Donald Trump and Tehran has led to the collapse of progress towards a peace settlement between Iran and the US.The recent missteps ended with Iran saying it would reinstate a complete block on the movement of commercial shipping through the strait of Hormuz and that it would not allow any of its stockpile of highly enriched uranium to be exported out of the country. Continue reading...
items.content: US president’s desperation for war to end has seen him trying to speed through a process he does not fully controlA set of mismanaged and premature media announcements by Donald Trump and Tehran has led to the collapse of progress towards a peace settlement between Iran and the US.The recent missteps ended with Iran saying it would reinstate a complete block on the movement of commercial shipping through the strait of Hormuz and that it would not allow any of its stockpile of highly enriched uranium to be exported out of the country. Continue reading...

items.title: Humanoid robots race past humans in Beijing half-marathon, showing rapid advances
items.standfirst: BEIJING — Dozens of Chinese-made humanoid robots showed off their fast-improving athleticism and autonomous navigation skills as they whizzed past human runners in a half-marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, highlighting the sector's rapid technical advances
items.content: BEIJING — Dozens of Chinese-made humanoid robots showed off their fast-improving athleticism and autonomous navigation skills as they whizzed past human runners in a half-marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, highlighting the sector's rapid technical advances

items.title: Humanoid robots show rapid advances as they race past humans in Beijing half-marathon
items.standfirst: Winning robot records faster time than Jacob Kiplimo’s world record More than 100 robots run in parallel tracks to avoid collisions with humansDozens of Chinese-made humanoid robots showed off their fast-improving athleticism as they whizzed past human runners in a half-marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, having lagged far behind a year earlier.The race’s inaugural edition last year was riddled with mishaps, as many robots struggled to get off the starting line, and most were unable to finish. Continue reading...
items.content: Winning robot records faster time than Jacob Kiplimo’s world record More than 100 robots run in parallel tracks to avoid collisions with humansDozens of Chinese-made humanoid robots showed off their fast-improving athleticism as they whizzed past human runners in a half-marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, having lagged far behind a year earlier.The race’s inaugural edition last year was riddled with mishaps, as many robots struggled to get off the starting line, and most were unable to finish. bullet_summary: In calls with Lebanese President ⁠Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, French President Emmanuel Macron condemned the "unacceptable attack", his ‌office said in a statement
Reported by BBC World, France24

items.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
items.standfirst: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.
items.content: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

items.title: French UNIFIL soldier killed in Lebanon, Hezbollah responsible, says Macron
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron announced the death of a French UN peacekeeping soldier Saturday following an attack that also left three people wounded in southern Lebanon, saying that "everything suggests" Hezbollah being responsible for the attack. The Iran-backed group denied involvement.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron announced the death of a French UN peacekeeping soldier Saturday following an attack that also left three people wounded in southern Lebanon, saying that "everything suggests" Hezbollah being responsible for the attack. The Iran-backed group denied involvement.

items.title: UNIFIL sheds light on southern Lebanon attack that killed French soldier
items.standfirst: A French soldier was killed in southern Lebanon on Saturday 18, while serving with UNIFIL, the United Nations peacekeeping force. UNIFIL deputy spokesperson Tilak Pokharel was FRANCE 24’s guest this afternoon to explain what happened and what is currently known about those responsible.
items.content: A French soldier was killed in southern Lebanon on Saturday 18, while serving with UNIFIL, the United Nations peacekeeping force. UNIFIL deputy spokesperson Tilak Pokharel was FRANCE 24’s guest this afternoon to explain what happened and what is currently known about those responsible.

items.title: French soldier killed in attack on UN mission in southern Lebanon, officials say
items.standfirst: A French soldier was killed and three others wounded ​while clearing a road in southern Lebanon in an attack that UNIFIL peacekeepers and French officials said on Saturday was likely carried out ​by ‌Iran-backed Hezbollah. In calls with Lebanese President ⁠Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, French President Emmanuel Macron condemned the "unacceptable attack", his ‌office said in a statement. Renée Davis, FRANCE 24's correspondent, has the latest from Beyrouth.
items.content: A French soldier was killed and three others wounded ​while clearing a road in southern Lebanon in an attack that UNIFIL peacekeepers and French officials said on Saturday was likely carried out ​by ‌Iran-backed Hezbollah. In calls with Lebanese President ⁠Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, French President Emmanuel Macron condemned the "unacceptable attack", his ‌office said in a statement. Renée Davis, FRANCE 24's correspondent, has the latest from Beyrouth.

items.content: A United Airlines flight from Chicago to New York was diverted to Pittsburgh Saturday afternoon. Passengers were forced to evacuate via emergency slides.

items.title: United plane makes emergency landing in Pittsburgh over ‘possible security issue’
items.standfirst: Passengers on flight from Chicago to New York exited via slides as FBI, bomb technicians and K-9 unit responded Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxA United Airlines flight bound for New York City was forced to land and evacuate in Pittsburgh on Saturday morning after crew members reported a “possible security issue”, a spokesperson for the Federal Aviation Administration said.United flight 2092 took off from Chicago O’Hare airport and had been en route to LaGuardia airport in New York. The plane diverted to Pittsburgh international airport as a result of the reported threat. Continue reading...

The plane diverted to Pittsburgh international airport as a result of the reported threat. Continue reading...

items.title: Two US army soldiers injured by brown bear during training exercise in Alaska
items.standfirst: Soldiers were participating in ‘land navigation training’ when bear injured them in a ‘defensive attack’Two US army soldiers have been injured after encountering a brown bear in a mountainous training area in Anchorage, Alaska, the military said on Friday.The incident happened on Thursday as the soldiers were participating in a “land navigation training event” in Arctic valley, part of the joint base Elmendorf-Richardson’s training area. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: U.S. sanctions two firms over allegedly recruiting Colombian mercenaries in Sudan war
clusters.neutral_headline: Sudan’s war leaves thousands of children alone and displaced
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, France24

items.title: U.S. sanctions two firms over allegedly recruiting Colombian mercenaries in Sudan war
items.standfirst: CAIRO — The U.S. imposed sanctions on three people and two firms over allegedly recruiting and deploying Colombian mercenaries to fight alongside the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces in Sudan's war, which has entered its fourth year with no end in sight
items.content: CAIRO — The U.S. imposed sanctions on three people and two firms over allegedly recruiting and deploying Colombian mercenaries to fight alongside the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces in Sudan's war, which has entered its fourth year with no end in sight

items.title: Sudan’s war leaves thousands of children alone and displaced
items.standfirst: Sudan is entering its fourth year of civil war between the army and the Rapid Support Forces, a conflict the UN calls the world’s largest humanitarian crisis, with children among the main victims. In Darfur and beyond, thousands are displaced, many separated from their families, facing hunger, violence and deep trauma despite overwhelmed aid efforts.
items.content: Sudan is entering its fourth year of civil war between the army and the Rapid Support Forces, a conflict the UN calls the world’s largest humanitarian crisis, with children among the main victims. In Darfur and beyond, thousands are displaced, many separated from their families, facing hunger, violence and deep trauma despite overwhelmed aid efforts.

items.title: Sudan war enters fourth year as the humanitarian crisis deepens
items.standfirst: Sudan has entered its fourth year of devastating conflict, after fighting broke out in April 2023 between the Sudanese army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. The war has left millions displaced and, according to the UN, created the world’s largest humanitarian crisis.
items.content: Sudan has entered its fourth year of devastating conflict, after fighting broke out in April 2023 between the Sudanese army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. The war has left millions displaced and, according to the UN, created the world’s largest humanitarian crisis.

items.content: Oil prices plunged and two U.S. stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels.
